message:
test(golden): refresh vfx_quake e1m1 checksums after stride-aware framebuffer change

The `vfx_quake-e1m1_120x40_16ms_seed42.checksums` golden file pins the
deterministic per-frame visual hashes for the Quake demo running at
120×40 cells, 16ms tick, seed 42, frames 001..006. The recently-landed
`perf(quake): size framebuffer to sampled stride, not declared resolution`
change (c7e21ed7) intentionally narrows the framebuffer the renderer
fills when stride > 1: instead of always rasterizing into a full
320×200 buffer, only the cells the painter will actually sample get
written. That changes the pixels that survive into the painter, which
in turn changes the per-frame checksum.

The visual output to the human eye is identical — the stride-skipped
pixels were never displayed before either; the renderer was just
computing them and discarding. But because the golden harness hashes
the framebuffer state, the on-disk checksums had to be regenerated to
match the new stride-aware buffer.

Refresh:

- `# Generated at:` timestamp bumps from 1775703211 to 1777414490.
- All six per-frame checksums (001..006) replaced with the
  post-perf-change values:
    001: 7082f618492cc2a8 → 26b98a7027a640f7
    002: 7bd8c8b33b52e727 → 2984d2efd4b68e01
    003: 6d8842a8ec2e41ce → 8d06db268c1b7d2f
    004: f901e1850e8a0e03 → 586783329f185b74
    005: 12e323e8adef5b65 → d24ab24f44522876
    006: 7b517f129e1a84cc → cfea3361591bdaa4

The values were regenerated by re-running the showcase golden suite
locally; the file's "do not edit manually" header is preserved
because the harness still owns the format.

This is the only golden checksum file affected by c7e21ed7 — the other
visual-effect goldens run at stride=1 (or are FX where the stride
clamp doesn't change the output region) and didn't drift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
